title: Pinterest Stock Drops on Revenue Miss and Light Guidance
text: Pinterest stock declined 3.9% following the release of its fourth-quarter 2022 earnings report. While adjusted earnings exceeded expectations, the company missed revenue projections and provided disappointing forward guidance. The social media platform generated $877.2 million in revenue, representing 3.6% year-over-year growth but falling short of Wall Street's $886.3 million consensus estimate. Adjusted earnings per share of $0.29 surpassed the expected $0.27. GAAP metrics showed significant declines: net income dropped 90% to $17.5 million, and EPS fell 88% to $0.03, compared to the prior-year period. Global monthly active users reached 450 million, up 4% year-over-year, while average revenue per user grew just 1% to $1.96 globally. Regional performance varied, with U.S./Canada users flat and European ARPU declining 9%. Management guided Q1 2023 revenue growth at approximately 1-3%, significantly below the 7.3% growth analysts had anticipated. The company attributed headwinds to reduced advertiser spending amid macroeconomic uncertainty. Additionally, CFO Todd Morgenfeld announced his departure in July, with a replacement not yet named.
